glossary.CDD: Config Driven Deployment — an architecture Swapnil proposed and implemented at Piramal to eliminate conditional hell, unify mortgage and non-mortgage loan flows, and make the app scalable across all loan products
glossary.FnB: Food and Beverages — one of the verticals in the Bajaj Finserv ONDC marketplace
glossary.ONDC: Open Network for Digital Commerce — government initiative for open e-commerce in India
glossary.CMS: Content Management System — Strapi was used as CMS for the EasyPay project
glossary.SEO: Search Engine Optimization — implemented sitemap.xml, robots.txt, and OG tags for the Bajaj Finserv project
glossary.MR: Merge Request — code review request raised on GitHub or Bitbucket before merging to main branch
glossary.HLSA: A mortgage-based loan product flow migrated to Config Driven Deployment
glossary.BLSA: A mortgage-based loan product flow migrated to Config Driven Deployment
glossary.PLSA: A non-mortgage loan product flow migrated to Config Driven Deployment
glossary.UBLTA: A non-mortgage loan product flow migrated to Config Driven Deployment
glossary.MFSA: A non-mortgage loan product flow migrated to Config Driven Deployment
glossary.UCL: A non-mortgage loan product flow migrated to Config Driven Deployment
glossary.SCUBL: A non-mortgage loan product flow migrated to Config Driven Deployment
glossary.LADS: A non-mortgage loan product flow migrated to Config Driven Deployment
glossary.ReviewGenie: An automated code review bot that runs on GitHub Pull Requests — flags code quality issues, pattern inconsistencies, and coverage gaps before human review

EasyPay.about: EasyPay was contracted by Bajaj Finserv Markets to build their ONDC marketplace — a multi-vertical e-commerce platform covering Food & Beverages, Grocery, and Electronics. This is a live production product.
EasyPay.myRole: Frontend Developer responsible for building the FnB, Grocery, and Electronics verticals from Figma to production — including Strapi CMS integration, API binding, SEO implementation, and performance optimization.
keyAchievements: Built 50+ components and pages from scratch using React and Next.js
Integrated Strapi CMS for dynamic content management across FnB and Grocery sections
Implemented SEO — sitemap.xml, robots.txt, OG image tags — as per Bajaj SEO audit report
Built dynamic search with auto-suggestions, pagination, and filters
Implemented location-based API integration for restaurant and grocery listings
Resolved 100+ UI and functional bugs across the platform
Added shimmer and skeleton loading states for better UX across all verticals
Implemented Config Driven CMS components for FnB, Grocery, and Electronics
Built mobile-first responsive UI with filter overlays and dynamic carousels
Presented SEO implementation to Bajaj Finserv team

Piramal - Sales Central.about: Piramal Sales Central is an internal tool used by loan sales officers to manage and track loan sanction applications. Swapnil joined as a frontend developer and quickly took ownership of a major architectural initiative.
Piramal - Sales Central.myRole: Frontend Developer responsible for bug fixes, feature development, and architecting the Config Driven Deployment system that replaced the old hardcoded SFDC flow — unifying mortgage and non-mortgage loan product flows into a single scalable architecture.
keyAchievements: Identified the core problem of conditional hell caused by separate mortgage and non-mortgage loan flows
Proposed, built POC, and presented Config Driven Deployment to Technical Director — received green flag
Architected and implemented CDD system that unified mortgage (HLSA, BLSA) and non-mortgage flows into one scalable architecture
Migrated 8+ loan product flows to the new CDD architecture
Reduced component duplication significantly across all product flows
Reduced time to fix bugs drastically by making the codebase config-driven and maintainable
Created comprehensive documentation, dev testing reports, and Product Journey flowcharts
Presented SCUCL demo to Testing team, UI team, and all developers
Implemented unit tests for all CDD related changes
Worked on Cloud Defence vulnerability fixes and Next.js version upgrades

BajajFinservMarkets.about: ONDC marketplace built for Bajaj Finserv Markets — a multi-vertical live production e-commerce platform covering Food & Beverages, Grocery, and Electronics.

FnBHome.description: Food and Beverages homepage — fully dynamic, CMS-driven, API-integrated page with dynamic search, location-based restaurant listings, carousels, and filters.
FnBHome.myContribution: Built the entire page from scratch — static UI from Figma, broke into reusable components, integrated Strapi CMS, bound all APIs, implemented debounced auto-search with pagination, location-based filters, and full mobile responsiveness.

GrocerySection.description: Grocery vertical — separate route with its own homepage, search, store listings, and cart logic.
GrocerySection.myContribution: Built Grocery homepage and routes, integrated Grocery APIs, added shimmer and skeleton loading, implemented store open/closed state logic to prevent invalid Add to Cart actions.

SEOImplementation.description: Full SEO implementation as per the official Bajaj Finserv SEO audit report.
SEOImplementation.myContribution: Implemented sitemap.xml, robots.txt, OG image tags, and meta tags for homepage and category pages. Resolved OG image issue for social media platforms. Presented full implementation to Bajaj team.

keyAchievements: Built 50+ components and pages from scratch
Integrated Strapi CMS for dynamic content management across FnB and Grocery
Implemented full SEO — sitemap.xml, robots.txt, OG tags — as per Bajaj audit report
Built dynamic search with debounced auto-suggestions, pagination, and filters
Implemented location-based API for restaurant and grocery listings
Resolved 100+ UI and functional bugs across the platform
Added shimmer and skeleton loading states across all verticals
Built mobile-first responsive UI with filter overlays and dynamic carousels

Protean.about: A separate client project worked on alongside the Bajaj project — involved building new UI pages, login forms, dashboard, and a ticket management system.
Protean.myContribution: Built banner carousel, login form, dashboard UI, raise and view ticket pages, captcha validation, Leading Agent UI, Help and Support API integration, and full mobile UI.

PiramalSalesCentral.about: Internal tool used by loan sales officers to manage and track loan sanction applications across multiple financial products.
PiramalSalesCentral.myContribution: Full ownership of the Config Driven Deployment architecture — identified the problem of conditional hell caused by separate mortgage and non-mortgage flows, proposed CDD solution, built POC, presented to Technical Director, got green flag, implemented across 8+ loan product flows, wrote unit tests, documentation, and dev testing reports.

ConfigDrivenDeployment.description: Config-driven architecture replacing the old hardcoded SFDC flow — unified mortgage (HLSA, BLSA) and non-mortgage flows into a single scalable system.
ConfigDrivenDeployment.impact: Migrated 8+ loan product flows, eliminated conditional hell, reduced bug fix time drastically, made future product additions plug-and-play. Completed March 13, 2026 — handed to QA for regression.

keyAchievements: Identified conditional hell caused by separate mortgage and non-mortgage loan flows
Proposed, built POC, and presented CDD to Technical Director — received green flag
Architected and implemented CDD system unifying all 8 loan product flows
Migrated 2 mortgage flows (HLSA, BLSA) and 6 non-mortgage flows to CDD
Reduced component duplication significantly across all product flows
Reduced bug fix time drastically through config-driven maintainability
Created comprehensive documentation, dev testing reports, and Product Journey flowcharts
Implemented unit tests for all CDD changes
Worked on Cloud Defence vulnerability fixes and Next.js version upgrades

stats.cddProjectStatus: Completed — handed to QA for regression on March 13, 2026

May-2025.summary: First month at EasyPay — onboarding, codebase exploration, and building the FnBHome page UI from scratch based on Figma designs. Ended the month with Strapi integration and first code review.

2025-05-12.description: First day at EasyPay — completed employee onboarding setup. Got access to company email, Figma designs, and Bitbucket repository. Explored the project structure to understand the codebase conventions.

2025-05-13.description: Set up the codebase locally. Went through the existing code to understand common utility functions, shared components, and project architecture patterns before starting feature work.

2025-05-14.description: Started building the FnBHome page — the main landing page for the Food and Beverages vertical. Implemented static UI based on Figma designs, keeping all components in a single page file as per initial instructions.

2025-05-15.description: Continued building FnBHome static UI — implemented remaining sections from Figma including hero banner, category carousel, and seller grid sections.

2025-05-16.description: Completed the FnBHome static UI — all sections from Figma implemented and pixel-accurate. Page ready for Strapi integration and API binding in the next phase.

2025-05-19.description: Updated FnBHome UI as per revised Figma designs shared by the team — multiple sections had layout and styling changes. Also got access to Strapi CMS to start understanding the content structure for integration.

2025-05-20.description: Continued UI updates as per the latest Figma design revisions — refined spacing, typography, and component layouts across multiple sections of FnBHome.

2025-05-21.description: Started Strapi CMS integration into FnBHome. Simultaneously refactored the single large page file into multiple smaller reusable components — improving maintainability and reducing lines of code per file. This component segregation was a key architectural improvement for future scalability.

2025-05-22.description: Created content assets in Strapi matching the Figma designs. Tested various edge cases in Strapi data and FnBHome components. Fixed missing React key prop issue in list-rendering components. Added null safety conditions to prevent crashes on missing data.

2025-05-23.description: Refactored CSS class naming conventions across FnBHome and all child components for consistency. Resolved feedback issues raised by manager in code review. Pushed updated code to Bitbucket and raised MR for team review.

2025-05-26.description: Tested HeroTopBanner and CategoryCarousel components thoroughly — found and resolved edge cases around empty data and missing images. Added dynamic End-to-End Banner Carousel to the FoodGrocerySellerGrid component with proper fallback handling.

2025-05-27.description: Refactored CSS and JavaScript logic as per team feedback. Added responsive toggle for carousel slider buttons — buttons are hidden on screens below 768px for better mobile UX.

2025-05-28.description: Refactored DoubleRowSellerCarousel, FnBSellerCardCarousel, and FoodGrocerySellerGrid components — improved code structure, removed redundancy, and made props more explicit. Pushed refactored code to Bitbucket.

2025-05-29.description: Implemented a filter overlay modal specifically for mobile viewports — improves UX by showing filters as a full-screen overlay on smaller screens instead of inline. Also reverted some code that was incorrectly removed in a previous push.

2025-05-30.description: Tested FnBHome across various Strapi data scenarios — empty data, partial data, and edge cases like missing images or null fields. Started preparing the page structure for full API binding integration in the next phase.

June-2025.summary: Heavy API integration month — dynamic search with auto-suggestions and pagination, All Restaurants with filter/sort/distance, location-based APIs, Recommended section, and production bug fixes. Also started FnBOrderTrack page.

2025-06-02.description: Started building the default search bar for FnBHome — the search UI that appears before the user types anything. Updated Strapi with new content from revised Figma designs.

2025-06-03.description: Integrated Strapi data into the FnBHome default search page. Added a central condition to toggle the search bar between Electronics and FnB verticals based on route context. Added navigation buttons for Electronics and FnB home pages. Started All Restaurants API integration.

2025-06-04.description: Identified a data mismatch in the All Restaurants API response — coordinated with the backend team and flagged the issues. Implemented Filter, Sorting, and distance calculation logic based on the API response. Adjusted UI as per feedback from backend team and seniors. Started simplification of HERO_TOP_BANNER Strapi component logic.

2025-06-05.description: Completed HeroTopBanner section — fully integrated with Strapi and all edge cases handled. Started building the Dynamic Search feature for the Food and Beverages vertical.

2025-06-06.description: Continued building Dynamic Search for FnB. Attempted to bind the Recommended Restaurants section API — data was not returning from backend. Resolved production bugs in the FnB section reported by the testing team.

2025-06-09.description: Resolved remaining production bugs in the FnB section. Completed Dynamic Search feature and pushed to Bitbucket for Team Lead review. Optimized FnBSellerGrid section — improved rendering performance and reduced unnecessary re-renders.

2025-06-10.description: Bound Recommended Restaurants API — backend data now returning correctly. Added No Data, Error, and Loading states for better UX. Added Cuisines filter to the All Restaurants API integration.

2025-06-11.description: Implemented page navigation on clicking Recommended Restaurant and All Restaurants cards — routing users to the correct restaurant detail pages. Started building the Order Details section UI.

2025-06-12.description: Continued working on the FnBHome search bar improvements. Updated Strapi content with new data from revised Figma designs shared by the team.

2025-06-13.description: Bound the Dynamic Search Results API and implemented Dynamic Auto-Search Suggestion — results update in real time as the user types. Implemented pagination for Dynamic Search Results to handle large result sets efficiently. Integrated the complete search UI with the API.

2025-06-16.description: Fixed navigation issue in the Default Search bar for FnB section — incorrect routing on search bar interaction. Added skeleton loader to the FnB search result page for better perceived performance. Updated View More button UI twice based on team feedback.

2025-06-17.description: Implemented loading state management in the FnB search result page. Fixed UI inconsistencies on FnBHome page. Started building the FnBOrderTrack page and its skeleton loading state.

2025-06-18.description: Continued building FnBOrderTrack page — implemented order status timeline UI and skeleton loading state for the page.

2025-06-19.description: Completed FnBOrderTrack page. Resolved bugs from the testing sheet — serial numbers 25, 31, and 53 — covering UI layout issues and functional edge cases.

2025-06-20.description: Fixed BannerCarousel, HeroCarousel, and NewHeroCarousel bugs — resolved rendering and data issues across all three carousel types. Made FnB APIs location-based — restaurant listings now update dynamically based on the user's current location.

2025-06-23.description: Continued resolving bugs from the testing sheet — addressed UI layout, spacing, and responsive design issues across FnB and Grocery sections.

2025-06-24.description: Continued resolving bugs from the testing sheet.
2025-06-24.detailNote: Add specific bug numbers or areas fixed if you remember

2025-06-25.description: Continued resolving bugs from the testing sheet.
2025-06-25.detailNote: Add specific bug numbers or areas fixed if you remember

2025-06-26.description: Continued resolving bugs from the testing sheet. Worked on scroll functionality and tile shifting logic in FnBHome — implemented smooth scroll behaviour and dynamic tile repositioning based on screen size.

2025-06-27.description: Continued bug fixes from testing sheet. Worked on overall app optimization — identified and resolved performance bottlenecks across FnBHome components.

2025-06-30.description: Started major route segregation — separated Electronics, Food and Beverages, and Grocery into independent routes and page structures. Created the Main Home page (entry point) and Grocery homepage as part of this vertical separation.

July-2025.summary: Route segregation completed, Grocery API fully integrated, shimmer/skeleton effects added, OpenKart new UI built, and heavy QA bug fixing across FnB and Grocery sections.

2025-07-01.description: Completed route segregation — created separate routes for FnB, Grocery, and Electronics verticals. Added content and data to Grocery and Main Home page components. Fixed minor UI issues and implemented route-related navigation functionality.

2025-07-02.description: Started Grocery section API integration — binding store listings, product data, and category APIs to the Grocery homepage components.

2025-07-03.description: Completed Grocery section API integration — all store listing, category, and product APIs successfully bound with proper loading, error, and empty states handled.

2025-07-07.description: Resolved issues found in Grocery section after API integration. Implemented Husky for automatic code formatting on pre-commit — ensuring consistent code style across the team. Added shimmer and skeleton loading effects across FnBHome, Grocery, and Main Home sections for better perceived performance.

2025-07-08.description: Started resolving new batch of UI bugs reported by QA team from the updated testing sheet — focused on layout, spacing, and visual consistency issues across FnB and Grocery sections.

2025-07-09.description: Continued resolving UI bugs from QA testing sheet across Food and Grocery sections — responsive design fixes, component alignment, and edge case handling.

2025-07-10.description: Continued resolving UI bugs from QA testing sheet across Food and Grocery sections.

2025-07-11.description: Continued resolving UI bugs from QA testing sheet across Food and Grocery sections.

2025-07-14.description: Continued resolving UI bugs from QA testing sheet — addressed remaining layout and functional issues across FnB and Grocery sections.

2025-07-15.description: Continued bug fixing from QA sheet. Cleaned up codebase by removing unused images and GIFs — reduced bundle size and improved load performance.

2025-07-16.description: Fixed navigation issue on the Search Result page — incorrect routing on certain search flows. Started CMS component integration for FnB, Grocery, and Electronics sections using Strapi.

2025-07-17.description: Added CMS content data for all three verticals in Strapi. Integrated CMS data into UI components and styled them as per Figma designs.

2025-07-18.description: Updated NoResultFound component conditions as per Figma — different states for FnB, Grocery, and Electronics. Resolved loading issue on Electronics search result page. Added conditional no-result image handling for Grocery section.

2025-07-21.description: Implemented new image for Electronics NoResultFound state. Updated error detail message for better user communication on empty search results.

2025-07-22.description: Researched ESLint integration with the existing Next.js project — explored configuration options, rule sets, and compatibility with existing Husky and Prettier setup.

2025-07-23.description: Started OpenKart New UI project — set up from Bajaj buyer codebase as the base, cleaned up unnecessary code, and made the initial commit on Bitbucket.

2025-07-24.description: Updated theming across the entire OpenKart website — replaced colors, icons, and brand assets throughout all pages to match the new OpenKart brand identity.

2025-07-25.description: Finalized icon and color updates across OpenKart. Prepared and verified the build for deployment.

2025-07-28.description: Resolved new batch of UI bugs from the testing sheet — addressed layout, spacing, and component behaviour issues across FnB and Grocery sections.

2025-07-29.description: Updated CSS for FnB Seller Landing Page and the no-result-found page — improved layout and visual consistency as per team feedback.

2025-07-30.description: Implemented business logic to prevent Add to Cart action when a store or restaurant is closed or not serviceable in the user's area. Added cursor pointer styling to all clickable elements across the app. Updated Shopping Cart UI and resolved a flickering effect caused by state update timing.

2025-07-31.description: Resolved bugs from the testing sheet. Started working on filter persistence logic and scroll condition handling for FnBHome — ensuring filters are retained on page scroll and navigation.

August-2025.summary: Modal refactoring, filter logic overhaul with appContext migration, SEO full implementation and Bajaj team presentation, and Protean project UI development alongside EasyPay work.

2025-08-01.description: Converted all OverlayModal instances across the app into the new Custom Modal component — standardizing modal behaviour and improving consistency. Updated filter page settings to work with the new modal structure.

2025-08-04.description: Built the Active Order popup carousel — shows active orders in a swipeable carousel when the user has ongoing orders. Integrated and tested the Delivery Agent calling system — allows users to call the delivery agent directly from the order tracking page. Replaced OverlayModal with Custom Modal in FnbCart and ShoppingCart components.

2025-08-05.description: Fixed issues in the Delivery Agent popup — resolved edge cases around agent availability, call button state, and popup dismissal behaviour.

2025-08-06.description: Updated filter logic to handle various user scenarios correctly — filters now persist properly across navigation. Migrated filter and restaurant data from session storage to appContext for more reliable state management across the app.

2025-08-07.description: Continued developing the filter and restaurant data persistence logic — ensuring selected filters and restaurant state survive page navigation and app context changes.

2025-08-08.description: Resolved all remaining filter logic issues across Home, Grocery, and FnB pages — filters now correctly persist, reset, and apply across all three verticals consistently.

2025-08-11.description: Researched SEO implementation for Next.js — explored robots.txt generation, sitemap.xml structure, and OG meta tag setup. Drafted implementation plan and shared for approval.
2025-08-11.aiUsedFor: Researching Next.js SEO best practices and sitemap generation approaches

2025-08-12.description: Continued SEO research — finalized implementation logic for robots.txt and dynamic sitemap.xml generation in Next.js. Documented the approach for team review.
2025-08-12.aiUsedFor: Researching sitemap hierarchy and Next.js metadata API patterns

2025-08-13.description: Resolved pending UI issues and pushed code to Bitbucket. Continued SEO research and implementation planning.

2025-08-14.description: Implemented full SEO setup as per the Bajaj Finserv SEO audit report — added meta tags, OG tags, and structured data for Homepage and all category pages. Set Mobile Phone category page as the default fallback for any category page not found in the CMS.

2025-08-18.description: Presented the full SEO implementation to the Bajaj Finserv team — walked through robots.txt, sitemap.xml, and OG tag setup. Resolved critical OG image tag issue where the image was not rendering on social media platform previews.

2025-08-19.description: Continued SEO refinements. Cloned the Protean project and built the Banner Carousel component as the first feature for the Protean UI.

2025-08-20.description: Built the new Protean login form UI, updated the dashboard layout, and started new page development for the Protean project.

2025-08-21.description: Built the Raise Issues page and View Tickets page for Protean. Created a UI modal for the Raise Tickets flow. Updated footer text and added logo to the Tickets page.

2025-08-22.description: Coordinated with team members to implement and validate captcha on the Protean login form. Built the full mobile UI for Raise Ticket and View Tickets pages — responsive layout for smaller screens.

2025-08-25.description: Removed 88 unused images from the EasyPay project — reducing bundle size significantly. Requested web-optimized image formats from the team to further improve load performance.

2025-08-26.description: Continued Protean UI development as per updated Figma designs shared by the team.

2025-08-28.description: Continued Protean UI development as per updated Figma designs.

2025-08-29.description: Implemented Dashboard UI changes as directed by team — updated layout, component positioning, and visual hierarchy.

September-2025.summary: Final month at EasyPay — SEO sitemap finalization, Protean project completion, Bajaj buyer enhancements, and knowledge transfer. Transitioned to Piramal Sales Central on September 30.

2025-09-01.description: Started working on sitemap.xml for product pages — researched sitemap hierarchy best practices for large e-commerce platforms with dynamic product URLs. Continued Protean UI development in parallel.
2025-09-01.aiUsedFor: Researching sitemap hierarchy for large e-commerce platforms with dynamic URLs

2025-09-02.description: Implemented sitemap.xml on localhost — tested dynamic product page URL generation and verified sitemap structure. Continued Protean UI development alongside.

2025-09-03.description: Continued Protean UI development as per updated Figma designs shared by team.

2025-09-04.description: UI corrections and new UI sections built as per directions from team — updated component layouts and visual hierarchy.

2025-09-08.description: Created new pages for the Protean project. Added new Electronics categories to the SEO implementation as per the latest Bajaj SEO audit report recommendations.

2025-09-09.description: Continued new Protean page development. Ran full test of the SEO implementation object — verified meta tags, OG tags, sitemap entries, and robots.txt across all page types.

2025-09-10.description: Built new Protean pages with updated popup design — implemented popup component with dynamic content and close behaviour.

2025-09-11.description: Continued Protean new pages and popup — refined animations, edge case handling, and mobile responsiveness.

2025-09-12.description: Completed remaining Protean pages. Added Service Card section to the Bajaj buyer frontend project — displaying key service offerings in a card grid layout.

2025-09-15.description: Continued Protean pages. Made small UI improvement in the Bajaj buyer project as per team feedback.

2025-09-16.description: Created Leading Agent UI for Protean and updated the logo. Integrated Help and Support section API in the Bajaj buyer project — displaying support tickets and contact options dynamically.

2025-09-17.description: Continued Help and Support API integration in Bajaj buyer project. Updated the Raise Tickets page table — improved column structure and data display.

2025-09-18.description: Made Protean UI improvements as per team feedback. Broke down large Bajaj buyer project components into smaller, more reusable pieces — improving maintainability.

2025-09-19.description: Continued Protean UI improvements and Bajaj buyer component refactoring.

2025-09-22.description: Continued Protean UI changes — final refinements to pages and components.

2025-09-23.description: Final testing and minor bug fixes in the Bajaj buyer project before handoff.

2025-09-26.description: Connected with Bajaj Finserv team to explain sitemap.xml functionality — walked through the structure, URL hierarchy, and update frequency. Discussed sitemap architecture to align with Bajaj's expectations.

2025-09-29.description: Explained full SEO implementation to team member — knowledge transfer session covering sitemap generation, robots.txt logic, and OG tag setup before transitioning to Piramal.

2025-09-30.description: First day at Greyamp Office — onboarding at Piramal Sales Central. Met the team and got an initial overview of the product and codebase.

October-2025.summary: Piramal onboarding — laptop delays, codebase setup, first bug fixes, and starting first feature development. Also helped teammates get tool access.

2025-10-09.description: Laptop received after waiting since October 1. Got access to Jira and Confluence. Awaiting GitHub repository access to start codebase setup.

2025-10-10.description: Got GitHub and repository access. Completed local codebase setup — installed dependencies, configured environment variables, and ran the project locally for the first time.

2025-10-13.description: Got assigned two regression issues on Jira. Coordinated with developers and QA testers to understand the user flow and successfully reproduce the issues in the local environment.

2025-10-14.description: Got two additional issues assigned. Completed first bug fix (JIRA SX-16008) — resolved the issue and raised a Pull Request on GitHub for review. Started investigating the second assigned issue.

2025-10-15.description: Continued working on the second assigned issue. Requested VPN and Postman access from the team to be able to test API calls against the development environment.

2025-10-16.description: Lower priority issues were deprioritized. Received VPN and Postman access. Moved to new feature development — created test leads in Postman to understand the proLogin authentication flow of the web app before starting implementation.

2025-10-17.description: Started feature development based on understanding of the proLogin flow — implementing UI components for the new feature.

2025-10-22.description: Continued feature development on JIRA SX-15073 — building out UI components and integrating with existing app structure.

2025-10-23.description: Continued feature development — implemented additional UI sections and connected them to the app state.

2025-10-24.description: Continued feature development — refined component logic and handled edge cases.

2025-10-27.description: Continued feature development with increased scope. Helped new team members get set up — assisted with access requests for Jira, Confluence, GitHub Copilot, and repository access.

2025-10-28.description: Continued feature development — working through implementation of remaining UI components.

2025-10-29.description: Continued feature development — refined implementation and resolved internal edge cases.

2025-10-30.description: Continued feature development — final implementation work before code review submission.

2025-10-31.description: Submitted code for review — pushed final changes to GitHub and raised MR for JIRA SX-15073. Started work analysis for the next ticket (JIRA SX-14966) to understand scope before starting development.

November-2025.summary: The landmark month — resolved pending feature and code reviews, then proposed, built, demoed, and got green flag for the Config Driven Deployment system. Full CDD implementation started by end of month.

2025-11-03.description: Started investigating JIRA SX-14966 — unable to reproduce the error in the normal flow, so mocked the required data locally to simulate the scenario and understand the root cause.

2025-11-04.description: Continued investigating JIRA SX-14966 with mocked data. Also received L1 review comments on JIRA SX-15073 (AutoPay user awareness feature) — started addressing the feedback.

2025-11-05.description: Completed UI development for JIRA SX-14966. Ticket put on hold as the error could not be reproduced by anyone on the team — mocked solution ready if issue resurfaces.

2025-11-06.description: Resolved L1 review comments on JIRA SX-15073 (AutoPay user awareness feature) — refined the popup behaviour, updated copy, and adjusted component logic as per feedback. Pushed updated code to GitHub.

2025-11-07.description: Received and resolved additional review comments on JIRA SX-15073 — further refinements to the AutoPay popup flow and button state logic. Pushed final changes. Manager asked to explore possibilities for restructuring and improving the overall app architecture.

2025-11-10.description: Resolved final MR comments and re-pushed SX-15073. Started analysis for the Config Driven Deployment POC — began mapping the existing post-login module structure to understand where conditional hell was originating and how a config-driven approach could unify the flows.
2025-11-10.aiUsedFor: Analyzing existing codebase structure and brainstorming config-driven architecture patterns

2025-11-11.description: Continued CDD analysis — mapped out all the conditional branches in the existing post-login flow to understand the full scope of the mortgage vs non-mortgage split. Identified that both flows shared common UI but had fundamentally different logic paths.
2025-11-11.aiUsedFor: Mapping conditional logic branches and exploring config schema design options

2025-11-12.description: Created a new concept project from scratch to demonstrate the CDD approach — a clean sandbox to prototype the config schema and component rendering logic without touching the production codebase.
2025-11-12.aiUsedFor: Prototyping config schema structure and dynamic component rendering approach

2025-11-13.description: Started full development of the CDD POC — built the config file structure, the dynamic component renderer, and the module dependency logic (e.g. AutoPay locked until Bank Details complete) all driven purely by config.
2025-11-13.aiUsedFor: Accelerating POC development with AI-assisted code generation and logic validation

2025-11-14.description: Presented the CDD idea and POC to the Technical Director and Manager — demonstrated how the config-driven approach eliminates conditional hell, unifies mortgage and non-mortgage flows, and makes adding new loan products plug-and-play. They liked the concept and showed interest in full implementation.

2025-11-17.description: Gave full CDD demo to the entire team — Technical Director, Manager, developers, and UI team all present. Presented the full concept, POC walkthrough, and implementation plan. Received green flag from the team to proceed with full implementation.

2025-11-18.description: Started implementing CDD in the production codebase — began with the post-login page. Created the initial config files defining module structure, dependencies, and rendering rules. Started removing the old hardcoded SFDC flow in parallel.

2025-11-19.description: Integrated the config files with the new UI components. Enhanced the config schema as new edge cases were discovered — ensuring the config was expressive enough to handle all module variations without code changes.

2025-11-20.description: Made all module action buttons fully dynamic via config — Start Now, Continue, and Edit buttons now read their behaviour, enabled state, and navigation target entirely from the config file. No hardcoded button logic remaining.

2025-11-21.description: Implemented the Pending Task bottom sheet opening logic — when a user has incomplete modules, the bottom sheet opens dynamically showing exactly which tasks are pending, driven entirely by config state.

2025-11-24.description: Refactored the Module Card component to be fully reusable — a single generic Module Card now replaces 4 separate product-specific Module Card components, driven purely by config props. Significant reduction in code duplication.

2025-11-25.description: Continued Module Card refactoring — tested the generic component across all product scenarios to ensure behaviour parity with the 4 components it replaced.

2025-11-26.description: Removed all product-specific and module-specific conditional logic from the Module Card component — moved every variation into the config file. The component is now completely product-agnostic and renders purely based on what the config provides.

2025-11-27.description: Updated the config structure to be scenario-based — config now handles different user journey scenarios (new user, returning user, partially complete) without any conditional logic in the components.

2025-11-28.description: Tested all button flows in the CDD implementation — Start Now, Continue, Edit, and Pending Task bottom sheet across multiple user scenarios and loan product types. Verified correct behaviour for both mortgage and non-mortgage flows.

December-2025.summary: CDD documentation, unit testing, merge requests, QA environment deployment, Cloud Defence vulnerability fixes, and extending CDD to cover more loan product flows.

2025-12-01.description: Created comprehensive testing documentation for the QA team — step by step test cases covering all CDD module flows and scenarios. Also created an Excel-based product document for SCUCL post-login flow so that QA team and new developers can understand the product without needing live access.

2025-12-02.description: Continued documentation — filled in missing sections of the product document and wrote the remaining code pieces that were pending from the CDD implementation.

2025-12-03.description: Created reusable Header and Footer components for module list rendering. Extracted the Geotagging logic out of the component and moved it into the config file — keeping the component clean and the geo logic declarative.

2025-12-04.description: Continued Geotagging logic extraction into config. Attended Revamping meeting and Sprint Retrospective — discussed CDD progress and upcoming sprint planning.

2025-12-05.description: Completed Geotagging logic extraction. Started incorporating RMPD (Risk and Mandate Processing Details) logic into the config file — abstracting another complex conditional flow out of the components.

2025-12-08.description: Attended Figma design session (11AM–12:15PM) — reviewed updated UI specs for post-login modules. Wrote unit tests for all CDD implementation changes. Prepared code for peer review.

2025-12-09.description: Walked through code with a senior team member for informal review before raising the MR — incorporated structural feedback on component organisation and config schema. Fixed failing unit tests that broke due to the structural changes.

2025-12-10.description: Updated documentation to reflect final implementation changes. Raised the Pull Request on GitHub for the CDD implementation — ready for formal L1 and L2 review.

2025-12-11.description: Created PR for the Esign ticket. Raised MR for the full UCL flow. Started working on Cloud Defence security vulnerabilities flagged in the project — identifying outdated dependencies and unsafe patterns.

2025-12-12.description: Confirmed new CDD code was successfully deployed to the environment. Continued Cloud Defence vulnerability fixes — updating dependency versions and resolving flagged security issues.

2025-12-15.description: Reverted Cloud Defence dependency updates — the versions suggested by Cloud Defence were incorrect and caused build failures. Rolled back to stable versions while investigating the correct fix.

2025-12-16.description: Worked on finalising the MR for the full Config Driven flow — addressing remaining structural and logic gaps before submitting for formal review.

2025-12-17.description: Continued finalising the MR for the Config Driven flow — completed final code cleanup and ensured all unit tests were passing before submission.

2025-12-18.description: MR created and submitted — waiting for formal review. Started working on the Product Journey flowchart — a visual documentation of the complete post-login user journey across all loan product flows.

2025-12-19.description: Received and resolved MR review comments — refactored config structure and updated component logic as per reviewer feedback. Pushed updated code to GitHub.

2025-12-22.description: Deployed CDD code to QA environment and ran thorough testing — verified all module flows, button states, dependency logic, and scenario-based config behaviour. Completed Product Journey flowchart documentation.

2025-12-23.description: Resolved Cloud Defence vulnerabilities with correct dependency versions. Updated Next.js version successfully. Verified all changes locally and merged MR to the main branch — CDD officially in main codebase.

2025-12-24.description: Worked on regression issues SX-17290 and SX-17532 — investigated, reproduced, and started fixing post-deployment regressions.

2025-12-26.description: Extended the config to support additional loan product flows — incorporating new module structures and dependency rules into the existing CDD architecture.

2025-12-29.description: Continued extending config for additional loan product flows — adding more product-specific module definitions and scenario configurations.

2025-12-30.description: Resolved additional Cloud Defence vulnerabilities. Updated Next.js version to latest stable. Verified all changes locally and created and merged MR successfully.

2025-12-31.description: Worked on APF web Cloud Defence vulnerabilities — investigating and resolving remaining security issues flagged in the APF web project.

January-2026.summary: Extending CDD to cover Buyback feature and additional loan product flows, multiple code review cycles, full team demo of SCUCL, and starting migration of remaining non-mortgage flows into the unified architecture.

2026-01-02.description: Resumed work after New Year — continued incorporating config changes for additional loan product flow support. Extended the config schema to handle new module dependencies and scenario variations.

2026-01-05.description: Started preparing the dev testing report for the Local and Dev14 platform — documenting test scenarios, expected behaviour, and edge cases for the QA team to validate the CDD implementation.

2026-01-06.description: Continued dev testing report preparation — completed test scenarios for all module flows and edge cases across Local and Dev14 environments.

2026-01-07.description: Raised MR for the latest config changes. Started working on the next feature ticket — initial scope analysis and codebase exploration.

2026-01-08.description: Received L1 review comments on the raised MR — started addressing feedback on config structure, component logic, and code organisation.

2026-01-09.description: Resolved L1 review comments and pushed updated code. Received L2 developer review comments — noted for resolution in the next session.

2026-01-12.description: Resolved all L2 review comments — refactored config schema and updated component rendering logic as per feedback. Re-pushed final code to GitHub.

2026-01-13.description: Started adding the Buyback feature to the CDD architecture — extended the config schema to support Buyback as a new loan product flow with its own module structure and dependency rules.

2026-01-14.description: Gave full demo of the CDD implementation to Testing team, UI team, and all developers — walked through the config structure, dynamic module rendering, dependency logic, and how new loan products can be added purely via config changes. Well received by all teams.

2026-01-15.description: Continued Buyback feature development — implemented Buyback specific module cards, button flows, and dependency rules in the config.

2026-01-16.description: Continued Buyback feature development — completed module flow and tested all Buyback scenarios against the config-driven renderer.

2026-01-19.description: Started migrating additional non-mortgage loan product flows into the unified CDD architecture — extracting product-specific logic and translating it into config-level definitions.
2026-01-19.aiUsedFor: Analyzing existing non-mortgage flow logic and translating conditional branches into config schema

2026-01-20.description: Continued migrating non-mortgage loan product flows — working through module dependency mapping and config schema definition for each product.
2026-01-20.aiUsedFor: Accelerating logic extraction and config translation for multiple product flows

2026-01-21.description: Continued migrating non-mortgage loan product flows into CDD — completed config definitions for another batch of product flows.
2026-01-21.aiUsedFor: Accelerating logic extraction and config translation for multiple product flows

2026-01-22.description: Continued non-mortgage flow migration — refined config schema for edge cases and tested module rendering across all migrated flows.

2026-01-23.description: Completed current batch of non-mortgage flow migration — all targeted flows successfully moved to the unified CDD architecture. Verified config-driven rendering across all migrated products.

2026-01-27.description: Fixed a bug introduced by config changes — Insurance Details module name was incorrectly mapped in the config, causing a display mismatch. Resolved by correcting the config definition.

2026-01-28.description: Provided dev testing proof — screenshots and test evidence for the QA team. Created and raised MR for the completed batch. Started analysis on the next set of loan product flows to migrate.

2026-01-29.description: Started migrating the next batch of loan product flows to CDD — began with analysis and config schema definition for each product's module structure and dependency rules.
2026-01-29.aiUsedFor: Analyzing loan product flow logic and translating into config definitions

2026-01-30.description: Integrated disbursement details and VKYC (Video KYC) module into the CDD architecture for a loan product flow — mapped their dependency rules and rendering logic into the config.

February-2026.summary: The mortgage unification stretch — migrating HLSA and BLSA (mortgage flows) into the unified CDD architecture alongside non-mortgage flows. The core technical challenge was that mortgage and non-mortgage flows had fundamentally different logic that needed to be abstracted into a single config-driven system.

2026-02-02.description: Continued VKYC and disbursement details integration for the loan product flow. Created and raised MR for the completed work.

2026-02-03.description: Started analysis on the mortgage loan product flows (HLSA and BLSA) — studied the existing flow logic to understand how mortgage-specific rules differ from the non-mortgage flows already migrated. The core challenge: mortgage flows had fundamentally different module structures, eligibility conditions, and dependency chains.
2026-02-03.aiUsedFor: Analyzing structural differences between mortgage and non-mortgage flows to design a unified config schema

2026-02-04.description: Started extracting mortgage flow logic — identifying all mortgage-specific conditional branches in HLSA and BLSA and translating them into config-level abstractions that could coexist with the non-mortgage config schema without breaking either flow.
2026-02-04.aiUsedFor: Extracting and abstracting mortgage-specific conditional logic into unified config definitions

2026-02-05.description: Continued mortgage flow logic extraction — worked through the eligibility and dependency logic unique to mortgage products and mapped them into the config schema. Ensuring the unified renderer handles both mortgage and non-mortgage without any product-specific code.
2026-02-05.aiUsedFor: Translating complex mortgage dependency chains into config-driven rules

2026-02-09.description: Continued mortgage flow migration — addressed edge cases where mortgage module dependencies conflicted with the existing non-mortgage config structure. Extended the config schema to accommodate mortgage-specific scenarios without breaking non-mortgage flows.
2026-02-09.aiUsedFor: Resolving config schema conflicts between mortgage and non-mortgage flow definitions

2026-02-10.description: Continued mortgage flow migration — refined the unified config schema to cleanly handle both flow types. The renderer now correctly distinguishes mortgage vs non-mortgage behaviour purely from config flags, with zero hardcoded product logic.

2026-02-11.description: Continued mortgage flow migration — completed config definitions for HLSA flow. Started BLSA config definition, mapping its module structure and dependency chain into the unified schema.
2026-02-11.aiUsedFor: Accelerating BLSA config definition and dependency chain mapping

2026-02-12.description: Received review comments on the MR covering multiple previously migrated non-mortgage flows — started addressing feedback on config structure, component prop handling, and edge case coverage.

2026-02-13.description: Resolved all review comments on the non-mortgage flows MR — updated config definitions and component logic as per feedback. Raised updated MR for further review.

2026-02-16.description: Resumed mortgage flow migration — continued BLSA config definition and started integrating both HLSA and BLSA configs into the main codebase alongside the existing non-mortgage flows.

2026-02-17.description: Continued mortgage flow integration — tested the unified renderer with both HLSA and BLSA configs active alongside non-mortgage configs. Verified that the same renderer correctly handles all flow types without any product-specific conditional logic.
2026-02-17.aiUsedFor: Validating unified renderer behaviour across mortgage and non-mortgage config combinations

2026-02-18.description: Continued mortgage flow integration — resolved rendering conflicts between mortgage and non-mortgage module structures. Finalized the config schema to cleanly support both flow types from a single unified definition.

2026-02-19.description: Continued mortgage and non-mortgage unification — with AI assistance analyzed the remaining conditional differences between the two flow types and abstracted them into config-level flags. The goal: one renderer, one schema, all products.
2026-02-19.aiUsedFor: Analyzing remaining conditional differences between mortgage and non-mortgage flows to finalize unified config abstraction

2026-02-20.description: Continued mortgage flow unification — implemented final config abstractions for the remaining edge cases. Both HLSA and BLSA flows now fully rendering through the unified CDD renderer alongside all non-mortgage flows.

2026-02-23.description: Continued mortgage and non-mortgage unification work — final testing and refinements to ensure all 8 loan product flows render correctly through the single unified CDD system.

2026-02-24.description: Completed the mortgage and non-mortgage flow unification — all 8 loan product flows (2 mortgage, 6 non-mortgage) now fully migrated and rendering through the unified Config Driven Deployment architecture. A single renderer, a single config schema, zero product-specific conditional logic in components.

2026-02-25.description: Created dev testing report for the mortgage flow migration — documented test scenarios for HLSA and BLSA flows including all module states, dependency chains, and edge cases. Completed UI transfer and updated Confluence documentation to reflect the final unified architecture.

2026-02-26.description: Continued dev testing report creation and documentation updates for the mortgage flows — added detailed flow diagrams and scenario tables to Confluence. Updated Google Sheets test matrix with HLSA and BLSA test cases.

2026-02-27.description: Finalized dev testing report, UI transfer documentation, and Confluence updates for the complete mortgage flow migration. All documentation ready for QA team handoff — covering the full journey from individual hardcoded flows to the unified Config Driven Deployment architecture across all 8 loan products.

March-2026.summary: Final sprint of the CDD project — resolving Review Genie automated review comments, getting the MR merged, fixing the Banner component broken by flow unification, increasing test coverage, and officially handing over to QA for regression testing.

2026-03-02.description: Started working on the MR for the final CDD batch — resolved automated review comments flagged by Review Genie. Addressed code quality, pattern consistency, and coverage issues before pushing for human review.

2026-03-04.description: Focused on getting the MR ready for merge — resolved remaining review comments, ensured all checks passing, and pushed final updates to GitHub.

2026-03-05.description: Continued focused effort on MR resolution — addressed any new review comments raised after the last push. Working towards getting the MR approved and merged as soon as possible.

2026-03-06.description: Continued MR resolution — final round of review comment fixes and code cleanup before approval.

2026-03-09.description: MR successfully merged. Discovered that the Banner component was affected by the CDD flow unification — the component's logic was tightly coupled to the old hardcoded flow and broke after the architectural change. Started analysis and prepared documentation to simplify and fix the Banner component logic.

2026-03-10.description: Resumed work on the Banner component fix — reviewed the documentation prepared yesterday and aligned with the team on the approach to simplify the Banner logic and make it compatible with the unified CDD flow.

2026-03-11.description: Completed the Banner component fix — simplified the logic to be compatible with the unified CDD architecture and got the code merged. Started working on increasing unit test coverage for the CDD components.

2026-03-12.description: Continued increasing unit test coverage for CDD components. Created MR for both the main branch and the release branch — ensuring the changes are ready for both ongoing development and the upcoming release.

2026-03-13.description: All CDD refactoring code successfully merged to both main and release branches. Documentation finalized and build verified. Officially handed over the entire CDD implementation to the QA team for full regression testing — marking the completion of the Config Driven Deployment project.
